title: The old gentleman on the train

lines.target: The train stopped at a station but no one got* on.
lines.base: El tren se paró en una estación pero nadie subió.
lines.pronunciation: .. trein... nou-uan... — 3 ... dehentélmén ... billérd... glaasis... — 4 ... koot... hevili. — 5 ... uaipt... straipt hankéchiif. — 6 ... eksept... — 7... obviósli... — 8 ... noutist... ridin... diskriitli. — 9 ... sheer... — 10 ... shou... — 11 ... ófendin... — 12 ...riicht... peich... térn... neibór... — 13 ... put...

lines.target: At the last minute, the door opened
lines.base: En el último momento, la puerta se abrió

lines.target: and an old gentleman with a grey beard and glasses got* into the compartment.
lines.base: y un anciano caballero con barba gris y gafas entró en el compartimento.

lines.target: I just caught it! he said, sitting down heavily.
lines.base: ¡Lo cogí por los pelos (justo)! dijo, sentándose pesadamente.

lines.target: He took* off his glasses and wiped his face with a striped handkerchief. (N. 1)
lines.base: Se quitó las gafas y se secó el rostro con un pañuelo a rayas.

lines.target: Everyone was reading* and smoking, except the old gentleman.
lines.base: Todo el mundo leía y fumaba, menos el viejo caballero.

lines.target: He had obviously had no time to buy* a paper before catching the train.
lines.base: Evidentemente no había tenido tiempo de comprar un periódico antes de coger el tren.

lines.target: David soon noticed that the man was reading* his paper with him, but very discreetly.
lines.base: David se dió cuenta enseguida de que el hombre estaba leyendo su periódico con él, pero muy discretamente.

lines.target: David's paper had twenty pages and he would have liked to share it with him,
lines.base: El periódico de David tenía veinte páginas y le hubiera gustado compartirlo con él,

lines.target: but he did not want to show the old gentleman that he had noticed he was reading* it:
lines.base: pero no quería mostrar al anciano caballero que se había dado cuenta de que lo estaba leyendo,

lines.target: he was afraid of offending him.
David reached the bottom of the page, but did not want to turn it because his neighbour was still reading*.
lines.base: David llegó al final de la página, pero no quería pasarla, porque su vecino estaba aún leyendo.

lines.target: At last, David solved the problem. He folded the paper, put it on the seat,
lines.base: Por fin, David resolvió el problema. Dobló el periódico, y lo puso en el asiento,

lines.target: closed his eyes and pretended to be asleep.
lines.base: cerró los ojos y simuló dormir (estar dormido).

gap_candidates.target: At the last minute, the door opened
and an old gentleman with a grey beard and glasses got* into the compartment. (1) (2) (3)
He took* off his glasses and wiped his face with a striped handkerchief. (N. 1)
Everyone was reading* and smoking, except the old gentleman. (5)
David soon noticed that the man was reading* his paper with him, but very discreetly.
but he did not want to show the old gentleman that he had noticed he was reading* it:
he was afraid of offending him.
